modules.name: CMS.Activities
modules.purpose: Activity logging infrastructure — defines activity models, logging service, modifiers, validators, filters, and the custom activity logger. THIS IS THE PRIMARY MODULE FOR DOC-3137.
publicInterfaces: IActivityModifier — Modify(IActivityInfo activity) — modifies activity data when logged
IActivityLogValidator — IsValid(IActivityInfo activity) — determines if activity is valid for logging
IActivityLogFilter — IsDenied() — filters activities before creation (no activity data access)
IActivityInfo — data contract with properties: ActivityContactID, ActivityType, ActivityURL, ActivityTitle, ActivityValue, ActivityChannelID, ActivityTrackedWebsiteID, etc.
ActivityEvaluationRegister (CMS.Activities.Internal) — singleton registry for Modifiers, Validators, Filters

activityLoggingDetail.registrationMechanism: ActivityEvaluationRegister.Instance.Modifiers.Register(() => new MyModifier()) — called from Module.OnInit() methods, NOT through RegisterImplementation/DI

loggingPipeline.Log: filters → create IActivityInfo → modifiers → validators → save → events

activityLoggingDetail.keyDifference_Filter_vs_Validator: IActivityLogFilter.IsDenied() has no parameters (cannot inspect activity data). IActivityLogValidator.IsValid(IActivityInfo) receives the activity and can inspect all properties.
activityLoggingDetail.keyDifference_Log_vs_LogWithout: Log() runs filters+modifiers+validators. LogWithoutModifiersAndFilters() runs ONLY validators. Cross-site tracking and certain form submissions use LogWithoutModifiersAndFilters.

modules.name: Kentico.Xperience.Admin
modules.purpose: Back-office administration UI — contains multiple sub-assemblies for admin framework, website management, digital marketing, commerce, and headless channels. Includes member/role management UI pages and web page security settings.

subModules.purpose: Member and member role management UI — listing, creating, editing member roles and members
publicInterfaces: MembersApplication — root UI application for member management
MemberRoleList — ListingPage for MemberRoleInfo objects, supports Delete action
MemberRoleEdit — InfoEditPage<MemberRoleInfo> for editing role properties (name, display name, description)
MemberRoleCreate — page for creating new member roles
MemberEdit — page for editing member properties
subModules.adminUINote: MemberRoleEdit only exposes MemberRoleName, MemberRoleDisplayName, MemberRoleDescription. No expiration settings in the admin UI for roles. Role expiration would require custom admin UI or custom object type forms.

subModules.purpose: Web page security settings UI — configure IsSecured flag and required member roles for web pages
publicInterfaces: WebPageSecurity — form page for setting content item security (IsSecured + RequiredMemberRoles) using IContentItemMemberRoleRetriever

modules.permissionRelevance: primary — implements permission evaluation and enforcement in admin UI

modules.name: CMS.ContentEngine
modules.purpose: Content item management — content types, content folders, content item CRUD, content queries, content language management, content publishing, and role-based content access control (ContentItemMemberRole subsystem).
publicInterfaces: IContentItemMemberRoleRetriever — GetMemberRoleIds(contentItemId), GetMemberRoleCodeNames(contentItemIds), GetAccessSettings(contentItemId, isSecured), GetAllMemberRoleCodeNames()
ContentAccessSettings — struct: IsSecured, RequiredMemberRoleIDs. Factory methods: Public(), Secured(), SecuredWithRoles(roleIds)
ContentItemRolesResultProcessor — IContentQueryResultProcessor that enriches query results with role names from ContentItemMemberRole bindings

contentAccessDetail.contentItemRoleBinding: ContentItemMemberRoleInfo binds content items to member roles — determines which roles can access secured content
contentAccessDetail.queryEnrichment: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor post-processes content query results to set ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames on IContentQueryDataContainer
contentAccessDetail.accessCheck: ContentAccessSettings.IsSecured + RequiredMemberRoleIDs determine access. Content queries need to respect roles when filtering results.
contentAccessDetail.cacheStrategy: ContentItemMemberRoleRetriever caches role lookups for 60 minutes with cache dependencies on MemberRoleInfo and ContentItemMemberRoleInfo

modules.name: CMS.Membership
modules.purpose: Core membership module — defines MemberInfo, MemberRoleInfo, MemberRoleMemberInfo (binding), RoleInfo, UserInfo, UserRoleInfo, authentication helpers, application permissions, and the MembershipModule. MemberRoleInfo is the role definition (cms.memberrole). MemberRoleMemberInfo is the member-to-role binding (cms.memberrolemember). CRITICAL: MemberRoleMemberInfo has NO expiration field — only MemberRoleMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberRoleID. Role expiration must be implemented via custom object types.
publicInterfaces: MemberInfo — cms.member info object: MemberID, MemberEmail, MemberEnabled, MemberCreated, MemberGuid, MemberName, MemberPassword, MemberIsExternal, MemberSecurityStamp
MemberRoleInfo — cms.memberrole info object: MemberRoleID, MemberRoleGUID, MemberRoleName, MemberRoleDisplayName, MemberRoleDescription. Defines role definitions for members.
MemberRoleMemberInfo — cms.memberrolemember binding: MemberRoleMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberRoleID. IsBinding=true. No expiration/validity fields.
RoleInfo — cms.role info object: RoleID, RoleName, RoleDisplayName, RoleDescription, RoleGUID. Used for admin/system roles.
UserRoleInfo — cms.userrole binding between UserInfo and RoleInfo (admin users)
SystemPermissions — constants for system permission codenames
modules.permissionRelevance: primary — defines SystemPermissions and the application-level permission evaluator

memberRoleExpirationAnalysis.expirationFields: NONE — MemberRoleMemberInfo only has MemberRoleMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberRoleID
memberRoleExpirationAnalysis.note: Task description confirms: role expiration requires a custom object type with MemberID, RoleID, ExpiresAt fields

modules.purpose: MVC web layer — 14+ projects for ASP.NET Core MVC integration including content delivery, membership, online marketing, page builder, visual builder, headless (GraphQL), and shared admin modules. Includes PagePermissionsChecker and FormPermissionsChecker for live-site permission enforcement.
publicInterfaces: CheckPagePermissionsAttribute — MVC filter attribute for page permission enforcement

subModules.purpose: Main MVC content delivery — page builder, form builder, email builder, preview, security (page/form permission checking), content retrieval.
Core MVC infrastructure — routing, application startup, authorization filters, content sync authorization.
ASP.NET Core Identity integration with Xperience membership system.
Online marketing MVC integration — A/B testing, personalization.
Headless delivery API — GraphQL via HotChocolate.
Platform-level ASP.NET Core integrations — routing, middleware.

modules.permissionRelevance: secondary — live-site permission checking via MVC attributes

modules.name: Kentico.Content.Web.Mvc
modules.purpose: MVC content delivery layer — content retrieval, web page routing, preview mode, secured content access validation for web channel requests.
publicInterfaces: ISecuredContentAccessValidator (internal) — CheckAccess(contentItemInfo, httpContext) returns ContentAccessCheckResult, CheckAssetAccess(assetMetadataItem, httpContext)
ContentItemFieldsSourceExtensions.HasAccess(IContentItemFieldsSource, ClaimsPrincipal) — extension method checking if user has access to content item based on IsSecured + RequiredMemberRoleNames
MemberAccessHelper (internal) — CheckAccess(isSecured, user, requiredRoles): Allowed/NotAuthenticated/Forbidden. Checks user.IsInRole() for each required role name.

securityAccessDetail.accessCheckFlow: SecuredContentAccessValidator.CheckAccess → gets requiredMemberRoleNames from IContentItemMemberRoleRetriever → MemberAccessHelper.CheckAccess checks user.IsInRole() for each role name
securityAccessDetail.roleExpirationImpact: When role expiration is implemented via scheduled task removing MemberRoleMemberInfo bindings, the IsInRole claim check will automatically deny access once the role is removed from the user. Content query results will still carry RequiredMemberRoleNames but the user won't match.

modules.name: ContentRetriever
modules.purpose: High-level content retrieval API — IContentRetriever interface and ContentRetriever implementation with extension methods for simplified calling patterns. Wraps ContentItemQueryBuilder with automatic caching, website context, and URL path handling.

modules.name: Kentico.Membership
modules.purpose: ASP.NET Core Identity integration — bridges Xperience membership objects (MemberInfo, MemberRoleInfo, MemberRoleMemberInfo) with ASP.NET Core Identity framework. Provides ApplicationUser, ApplicationRole, ApplicationUserStore (IUserRoleStore<TUser>), ApplicationRoleStore (IRoleStore<TRole>), and services for member management including admin identity.
publicInterfaces: ApplicationRole — IdentityRole<int> wrapper around MemberRoleInfo: MapFromMemberRoleInfo/MapToMemberRoleInfo for bidirectional mapping. Properties: Id, Name, DisplayName, Description
ApplicationRoleStore<TRole> — IRoleStore<TRole> implementation: CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync, FindByIdAsync, FindByNameAsync using IInfoProvider<MemberRoleInfo>

ApplicationUserStore<TUser> — IUserRoleStore<TUser> implementation: AddToRoleAsync creates MemberRoleMemberInfo binding, RemoveFromRoleAsync deletes it, GetRolesAsync, IsInRoleAsync. Also implements IUserPasswordStore, IUserEmailStore, IUserLoginStore, IUserSecurityStampStore
AdminApplicationUser — IdentityUser<int> wrapper for admin users (UserInfo)
AdminApplicationUserStore — admin user store backed by UserInfo/RoleInfo

roleManagementDetail.addToRole: ApplicationUserStore.AddToRoleAsync — resolves MemberRoleInfo by name, creates MemberRoleMemberInfo binding (no expiration support)
roleManagementDetail.getRoles: ApplicationUserStore.GetRolesAsync — queries MemberRoleMemberInfo joined with MemberRoleInfo for a member
roleManagementDetail.isInRole: ApplicationUserStore.IsInRoleAsync — checks existence of MemberRoleMemberInfo binding

modules.name: CMS.Scheduler
modules.purpose: Task scheduling framework — provides IScheduledTask interface, RegisterScheduledTaskAttribute for registration, ScheduledTaskConfigurationInfo for task metadata, SchedulerBackgroundService for execution, and built-in tasks like EmailAttachmentCleaner and TemporaryUploadsCleaner.
publicInterfaces: IScheduledTask — Execute(ScheduledTaskConfigurationInfo task, CancellationToken) returns ScheduledTaskExecutionResult. THE interface custom scheduled tasks must implement.
RegisterScheduledTaskAttribute — [assembly: RegisterScheduledTask(identifier, typeof(TaskImpl))] — registers a scheduled task with a unique identifier
ScheduledTaskConfigurationInfo — task metadata: name, interval, enabled status, last run time, etc.
ScheduledTaskExecutionResult — result of a scheduled task execution
ITaskCancellationService — service for cancelling running tasks

scheduledTaskDetail.customTaskPattern: 1. Create class implementing IScheduledTask, 2. Register with [assembly: RegisterScheduledTask('identifier', typeof(Task))], 3. Create task configuration via admin UI (setting interval, enabled, etc.)
scheduledTaskDetail.executionModel: SchedulerBackgroundService runs as ASP.NET Core hosted service, checks for due tasks, resolves IScheduledTask implementations by identifier, calls Execute()
scheduledTaskDetail.roleExpirationUseCase: Custom scheduled task would: query custom object type for expired role assignments (ExpiresAt < DateTime.Now), then remove corresponding MemberRoleMemberInfo bindings via IInfoProvider<MemberRoleMemberInfo>

permissionArchitecture.summary: Three-layer permission model: (1) Application-level permissions via SystemPermissions + ApplicationPermissionEvaluator (View, Update, Create, Delete), (2) Workspace-level permissions via WorkspaceDataPermissions + WorkspaceDataPermissionEvaluator (View, Create, Update, Delete, Synchronize), (3) Page-level ACL permissions via WebPageAclPermissions + WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator (Display, Read, Create, Update, Delete, Synchronize). Administrators bypass all checks. Channel admins bypass ACL checks for their channel.
evaluatorChain: IWebPageAclPermissionEvaluator — page-level ACL (Admin.Base interface, Admin.Websites implementation)
keyBehaviors: Channel admins bypass ACL checks for their channel
WebPageAclPermissions.DISPLAY is implicitly assigned to all roles
READ and DISPLAY are individually checked (IndividuallyCheckedPermissions)
Non-READ/DISPLAY permissions also require READ permission check
ACL permissions can be evaluated recursively on child pages (evaluateChildPages flag)
Permission results are cached per-request in WebPageAclPermissionEvaluator
ContentTab checks WebPageAclPermissions.UPDATE and sets FormEditMode.ReadOnly if denied
WebPageBase checks WebPageAclPermissions.READ as base page requirement

roleExpiration.summary: Xperience by Kentico has NO built-in role expiration mechanism. MemberRoleMemberInfo (the binding between members and member roles) contains only three fields: MemberRoleMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberID, MemberRoleMemberMemberRoleID — no expiration date, no ValidTo, no time-bound assignment. Role expiration must be implemented as a custom solution using: (1) a custom object type with MemberID, RoleID, ExpiresAt fields, (2) a custom scheduled task implementing IScheduledTask that periodically checks for expired role assignments and removes the corresponding MemberRoleMemberInfo bindings, and (3) potentially updated content queries that respect the ExpiresAt date.
keyTypes: MemberRoleInfo (CMS.Membership) — role definition: MemberRoleID, MemberRoleName, MemberRoleDisplayName, MemberRoleDescription
MemberRoleMemberInfo (CMS.Membership) — member-to-role binding: MemberRoleMemberMemberID → MemberRoleMemberMemberRoleID. No expiration fields.
MemberInfo (CMS.Membership) — member definition: MemberID, MemberEmail, MemberEnabled, MemberName
ContentItemMemberRoleInfo (CMS.ContentEngine.Internal) — content item to role binding for content access control
ContentAccessSettings (CMS.ContentEngine) — struct: IsSecured + RequiredMemberRoleIDs for content access checks
ApplicationRole (Kentico.Membership) — ASP.NET Core Identity wrapper around MemberRoleInfo
ApplicationUserStore (Kentico.Membership) — IUserRoleStore with AddToRoleAsync/RemoveFromRoleAsync managing MemberRoleMemberInfo bindings
IScheduledTask (CMS.Scheduler) — interface for custom scheduled tasks
RegisterScheduledTaskAttribute (CMS.Scheduler) — attribute for registering custom scheduled tasks
keyFiles: CMSSolution/Membership/Members/MemberRoleMemberInfo.cs — THE binding type that needs expiration tracking (currently has none)
CMSSolution/Membership/Members/MemberRoleInfo.cs — role definition (no expiration on roles themselves)
CMSSolution/Scheduler/Tasks/IScheduledTask.cs — interface custom expiration tasks must implement
CMSSolution/ContentEngine/ContentItemMemberRole/ContentItemMemberRoleRetriever.cs — retrieves role bindings for content access control
CMSSolution/ContentEngine/Query/Executor/ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs — enriches content queries with role names

mechanisms.name: Role assignment
mechanisms.description: Members are assigned to roles via MemberRoleMemberInfo binding objects. ApplicationUserStore.AddToRoleAsync creates a new MemberRoleMemberInfo with MemberID + MemberRoleID.

mechanisms.name: Role removal
mechanisms.description: ApplicationUserStore.RemoveFromRoleAsync deletes the MemberRoleMemberInfo binding. A scheduled task for expiration would use IInfoProvider<MemberRoleMemberInfo>.Delete() to remove expired bindings.

mechanisms.name: Content access check
mechanisms.description: When content is secured (ContentItemIsSecured=true), the system checks if the current user has any of the required member roles (ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames). MemberAccessHelper.CheckAccess calls user.IsInRole() for each required role. Access is denied if the user doesn't have any required role.

mechanisms.name: Content query enrichment
mechanisms.description: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor (IContentQueryResultProcessor) post-processes content query results to populate ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames on IContentQueryDataContainer. This enables client-side access checks on query results.

mechanisms.name: Scheduled task framework
mechanisms.description: Custom scheduled tasks implement IScheduledTask.Execute(). Tasks are registered via [assembly: RegisterScheduledTask('identifier', typeof(TaskImpl))]. Task configuration (interval, enabled) is managed in admin UI via ScheduledTaskConfigurationInfo.

mechanisms.name: Custom object type framework
mechanisms.description: Custom object types extend AbstractInfo<TInfo, TProvider> and register via [assembly: RegisterObjectType]. The DataEngine provides IInfoProvider<T> for CRUD operations. A role expiration custom object type would follow this pattern with fields for MemberID, RoleID, ExpiresAt.

documentationImplications: New page: membership role expiration scenario — custom object type + scheduled task pattern
Content retrieval docs: mention that content queries respect role assignments, and expired roles (once removed by scheduled task) automatically deny access
Scheduled tasks docs: link to role expiration as a practical use case
Custom object types docs: link to role expiration as a practical use case
Security settings docs: clarify that role-based content access relies on MemberRoleMemberInfo bindings existing — removing the binding removes access
